'''Marja Lang''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Lieutenant governor/Legends|Vice-Governor]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. A staunchly loyal Imperial, she was more dedicated to the ideals of the Empire than [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the [[Sector/Legends|sector's]] ruler. When Sarne withdrew the sector from the Empire in the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and established himself as a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], Lang's devotion to the Empire ensured that she was frozen out of the personality cult that Sarne developed among his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] that engendered loyalty to him over the Empire. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|disposed]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. He retreated to Gandle Ott with his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], stripping the [[Ott system|system]] of its [[Gandle Ott system defense force|defense force]] and [[Unidentified Imperial Governor (Gandle Ott)|governor]] before departing.

With the planet in confusion after Sarne's departure, Lang found herself the highest [[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] Imperial on the planet and moved to secure a power base with her fellow abandoned Imperials with the hopes that she would ascend to the post of [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. However, her position was challenged by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Herron Dade]], the native [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Gandle Ott planetary militia|planetary militia]]. Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the Moff's departure, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' entered [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of Sarne. A delegation from the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] spent several days on the planet establishing [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] ties, and although Lang was not especially welcoming of their presence, she understood the need to treat them pleasantly.

==Biography==
Marja Lang was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrat]] who was stationed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] as the [[Lieutenant governor/Legends|Vice-Governor]]. A capable [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]], Lang was loyally devoted to the Imperial philosophy.<ref name="dirp" /> However, when the ruler of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], quietly withdrew his holdings from the Empire in the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and established himself as a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]],<ref name="tdc">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref> he began to cultivate a personality cult within his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]], engendering total loyalty to himself. Due to Lang's fervent loyalty to the Empire proper, she was excluded from the clique.<ref name="dirp" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|deposed]] Sarne and liberated his [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]]. Sarne fled with the majority of his forces, stopping at Gandle Ott to take on supplies. The Moff claimed to have assembled a large [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to deal with [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] throughout the sector, and he made no mention of his recent defeat. Upon his departure, Gandle Ott's citizens discovered that Sarne had left with the majority of Imperial personnel stationed on the planet—including the planetary [[Unidentified Imperial Governor (Gandle Ott)|governor]]—and the local [[Gandle Ott system defense force|defense force]]. With the [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] gone, Lang became the highest-[[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] Imperial official on the planet and took on the title of Acting-Governor.<ref name="dirp" />

With much of the Imperial command structure removed, Gandle Ott fell into confusion. The [[Bank parliament]], the civilian government that had ruled the planet prior to the institution of Imperial authority, began to retake power. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Herron Dade]], [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Gandle Ott planetary militia|planetary militia]] and highest-ranking native-[[Reproduction/Legends|born]] official, started to position himself to make a run for the long-abolished [[President/Legends|presidency]]. When [[NewsNet]] [[Courier droid/Legends|courier droids]] reached Gandle Ott bearing reports that the New Republic had ousted Sarne from Kal'Shebbol, Lang began to consolidate her own power base among the remaining Imperial forces and sent [[Subspace/Legends|subspace]] messages to the Empire requesting reinforcements. Lang hoped that she would officially be named as governor of Gandle Ott. The civilian authorities, fearful that the New Republic would turn its attention on Gandle Ott, made it known that they would be interested in negotiations with the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] power.<ref name="dirp" />

[[File:FarStar_delegation.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Marja Lang leads the Gandle Ott delegation in welcoming the command crew of the ''FarStar''.]]
Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after Sarne's departure, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived in Gandle Ott's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of the Moff. The [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] was permitted to land at [[Montrol Spaceport]], and Lang—along with [[Stanfeld Duffit]], [[Mayor/Legends|Mayor]] of [[Montrol City]]; several [[Politics/Legends|politicians]]; and an [[honor guard]]—headed to the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] to formally greet the New Republic representatives. Upon arrival, Lang discovered that the spaceport's security force was intent on arresting the ''FarStar'' crew for piracy. A standard check of the ship's registration and [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|Bureau of Ships and Services]] permits listed the ''FarStar'' as stolen Imperial property. Unknown to Lang, Montrol [[City/Legends|City's]] [[BRT supercomputer/Legends|BRT supercomputer]], [[CUTH-BRT-92-X3]], had been corrupted by Sarne with instructions to delay any New Republic pursuit. To that end, CUTH-BRT-92-X3 altered the records to flag the ''FarStar'' as stolen. Lang resolved the issue promptly, and formally welcomed the ''FarStar'' delegation, led by the ship's [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], to Gandle Ott. She then invited the senior staff to accompany her to the [[Imperial Plaza Hotel]], where they would stay for the duration of their visit at the government's expense. Before leaving the delegation at the [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]], Lang invited them to join her for an informal breakfast with her and the [[Imperial Cabinet]].<ref name="dirp" />

Among the ''FarStar'' delegation that Lang met was [[Jessa Dajus]], a former [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] who had served in Sarne's forces. Dajus had fallen out of the Moff's favor and had been discovered by New Republic forces in his [[Detention area|detention cells]], where she claimed to be a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. When the ''FarStar'' mission was authorized, Dajus was allowed to join the crew as a tactical advisor. Lang had met Dajus before, when she was serving with Sarne, but did not reveal that fact in public. Lang planned to get Dajus alone and determine whether or not she was truly sympathetic to the New Republic or if she was playing her own game.<ref name="dirp" />

On the second day of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} visit, the delegation accepted Lang's offer and joined her and the Cabinet for breakfast. Several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, Lang joined General Dade at the [[Ceremonial Mushroom Cutting]], where he symbolically declared that Gandle Ott had renounced its ties to the Empire and should look to controlling its own future. Dade was the center of attention for the gathered [[Media/Legends|media]], and Lang was barely able to conceal her boredom during the ceremony. On the third day, Lang hosted a private lunch for the ''FarStar'' delegation and her key supporters. After several days on the planet, during which diplomatic relations were established between Gandle Ott and the New Republic, the ''FarStar'' departed to continue its mission.<ref name="dirp" />

==Personality and traits==
Marja Lang was a short, pudgy woman with [[Color/Legends|blonde]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that she wore in a long braid down her back. Generally even-tempered, Lang could be charming or threatening when the need arose. Although she did not like dealing with the New Republic, she saw that treating them as equals was wise, and was polite and civil to the delegation. A lifelong bureaucrat, Lang was well-versed in the workings of the Imperial government, and she was knowledgeable about businesses and other cultures. She could operate [[Comm unit/Legends|communications]] and security equipment, and wore an Imperial uniform on duty. She carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and personal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="dirp" />

==Behind the scenes==
Marja Lang was introduced in ''[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]'', a [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] adventure published in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. During the course of the adventure, the players have to deal with CUTH-BRT-92-X3's attempts to delay the ''FarStar'' from pursuing Sarne, which manifests itself in several ways, including near fatal traffic accidents and an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] being unleashed on them. Lang, along with Dade, is one of the officials to whom the players can report these occurrences, along with the suspicion that CUTH-BRT-92-X3 is responsible. Lang takes the information seriously and will look into the matter and discover the protocol modifications made to the unit by Moff Sarne. CUTH-BRT-92-X3 is taken offline and [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] are employed to restore the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to normal. As the players can talk to either Lang or Dade, this article makes no assumption as to which official receives the reports.
